source_summary: Existing first-pass hub Ngram frequency series.
Targeted Google Books Ngram requests for missing routing terms where available.
limitations: Google Books Ngram series begins at 1800 and ends at 2022.
Ngram values are printed-book frequency proxies, not direct semantic proof.
Search-result counts are not used as exact frequency.
Missing or failed query terms are retained with status and failure notes.
Group sums are routing proxy aggregates, not exact sense counts.
First nonzero Ngram year is not a historical first attestation.
Regional/global/institutional terms are kept separate because routing sense can be ambiguous.

routing_layers.label: Rail / Transit Route
routing_layers.summary: Hub as a node in rail, transit, passenger, and urban route systems.

period_visibility.notes: Available Ngram data begins at 1800, so this bucket is effectively 1800-1849.
Final bucket is limited to 2020-2022 because the current Ngram corpus ends at 2022.

routing_layers.label: Air / Logistics Route
routing_layers.summary: Hub as a node in aviation, shipping, logistics, distribution, and supply-chain movement.

period_visibility.notes: Available Ngram data begins at 1800, so this bucket is effectively 1800-1849.
Final bucket is limited to 2020-2022 because the current Ngram corpus ends at 2022.

routing_layers.label: Hub-and-Spoke Model
routing_layers.summary: Explicit model language that turns hub/spoke geometry into routing architecture.

period_visibility.notes: Available Ngram data begins at 1800, so this bucket is effectively 1800-1849.
Final bucket is limited to 2020-2022 because the current Ngram corpus ends at 2022.

routing_layers.label: Network / Communication Route
routing_layers.summary: Hub as a node for signal, communication, computer-network, and data routing.

period_visibility.notes: Available Ngram data begins at 1800, so this bucket is effectively 1800-1849.
Final bucket is limited to 2020-2022 because the current Ngram corpus ends at 2022.

routing_layers.label: Institutional Route Language
routing_layers.summary: Access/distribution hub language in institutions and services; kept as a boundary layer.

period_visibility.notes: Available Ngram data begins at 1800, so this bucket is effectively 1800-1849.
Final bucket is limited to 2020-2022 because the current Ngram corpus ends at 2022.

evidence_items.evidence_text_short: Derived-term inventory includes hub-and-spoke, hubbed, hubbing, hubcap, and datahub.
evidence_items.context_summary: Paraphrased derived-term inventory.
evidence_items.limitations: Existing first-pass snippet; not newly scraped for Chart 02.
evidence_items.copyright_notes: Short paraphrase or short snippet retained for research planning only.

evidence_items.context_summary: Defines hub-and-spoke as an air-traffic routing system and lists first known use as 1980.
evidence_items.limitations: Dictionary claim for a specific transport-system adjective; no quotation visible in the public entry.
evidence_items.copyright_notes: Short paraphrase or short snippet retained for research planning only.

evidence_items.evidence_text_short: Modern dictionary sense includes a transport interchange and activity center.
evidence_items.context_summary: Paraphrased modern dictionary sense.
evidence_items.limitations: Existing first-pass snippet; not newly scraped for Chart 02.
evidence_items.copyright_notes: Short paraphrase or short snippet retained for research planning only.

evidence_items.source_title: Image 6 of The evening world (New York, N.Y.), May 16, 1908, (Final Results Edition)
evidence_items.evidence_text_short: IftG cOL I Ilrr 1 I i y b THE EVENING WORLD SATURDAY h MAY 16 19OS I NEW TRANSIT HUB i IS TO...
evidence_items.context_summary: loc.gov OCR description includes transit hub.
evidence_items.limitations: Readable OCR search-result description; page image should be checked before final use.
evidence_items.copyright_notes: Short paraphrase or short snippet retained for research planning only.

evidence_items.source_title: Image 8 of The Washington daily news (Washington, D.C.), December 3, 1943
evidence_items.evidence_text_short: United Press Stitt Correspondent MOSCOW Dec 3The Rus sians have smashed into the northeastern suburbs of Znam enka strategic railway hub con trolling one of the main German escape routes from the Dnieper Bend front reports said today Stepping up his attack sou
evidence_items.context_summary: loc.gov Chronicling America OCR description; verify page image before final copy.
evidence_items.limitations: Existing first-pass snippet; not newly scraped for Chart 02.
evidence_items.copyright_notes: Short paraphrase or short snippet retained for research planning only.

data_cautions: Ngram is printed-book frequency, not semantic proof.
Group sums are routing proxy aggregates, not exact sense counts.
Regional, global, international, business, digital, and platform hub terms can be ambiguous without context.
The latest Ngram bucket ends at 2022.
First Ngram appearance is not first historical attestation.

hardened_evidence.context_summary: Defines hub-and-spoke as an air-traffic routing system and lists first known use as 1980.

core_model_status.notes: Core model remains supported by the two existing hub-and-spoke frequency terms; expanded variants remain gap-limited.

routing_layers.notes: Use failed or ambiguous query variants only as cautions.

remaining_gaps.gap: Expanded hub-and-spoke variants still lack recovered frequency series.
remaining_gaps.notes: Core terms hub and spoke / hub-and-spoke remain available and strong enough for the model.

recovery_summary.main_model_status: Core model remains supported by the two existing hub-and-spoke frequency terms; expanded variants remain gap-limited.
recovery_summary.notes: Network-dependent frequency recovery remained transparent; no absent series were invented.

remaining_gaps.gap: Expanded hub-and-spoke variants still lack recovered frequency series.
remaining_gaps.notes: Core terms hub and spoke / hub-and-spoke remain available and strong enough for the model.
